textByHash.b309f7672935e14207ac2dbde229b5824bd35d7d: I bet people would get pretty angry if they had their food taken away. You could burn the food stores in West Ardougne and claim it's to protect people from the plague.
textByHash.e609a551df6c3a8379789a41708bc914f4a9d9cf: Some will, but don't forget that it was the authorities that supplied the food in the first place. People will soon get angry if they think that not enough care is being taken with the food.
textByHash.cc7b22956ad38b4e3888654c2e7027aaabc80d46: My son fell ill with the plague...
textByHash.37e788c8e2befaa29b07d870e07aa59e5f540ac2: It would be easier to cope with if I could have spent his last few days with him.
textByHash.94ab0e419ef5665991330ea1885584b4ffef93e6: Those mourners came and whisked him away. He didn't even seem that ill, I thought it was a common cold... But the mourners said that he was infected and had to be taken away.
textByHash.0cc720938df5e6aa3601844e84d8c1517ebfabad: Two days later the mourners returned and told me he had died.
textByHash.8920f4b20b4de0c6431cd1fd8115574496fbc643: It's down to King Tyras. He and his men brought the plague here from the west, and then left us to suffer. One day he'll pay for what he's done!
textByHash.216c04dac6df1cf9561fbc8cec914da31e3bfbf5: You could say that... I've heard reports that half of West Ardougne is infected! Many have lost friends and family...
textByHash.5cd2aa5e53b756d79a01233796b206890afcacf3: People are very depressed and scared. I've never met anyone fully infected though. I suppose we should be grateful to the mourners for that.
textByHash.57d2053e6592fdf2e25c45597ab1e1df9dea7880: I'm okay. The loss of my son is something that I'll never be over. At least no one else has to suffer at the hands of those mourners though.
